coordinate_normalization.description: Coordinates are centered at the current projected center of mass in the view. The scale is the larger of the maximum absolute x-extent and y-extent from that center, and is set to 1 for both axes.
coordinate_normalization.note: The coordinate scale is recomputed from each updated observation.

rotation_convention.note: Clockwise rotation in the current image plane is theta_z = -90.

command_invocation_format.description: Each command is represented by command_name and parameters.

command_return_format.description: Each command returns executable. If executable is 0, the physical state is unchanged.

format.observation: Updated observation if the accepted command changes the state; otherwise the current observation.

fold_info_schema.description: A fold_info is a self-contained specification of one fold.

required_fields.name: crease_line_edge1
required_fields.description: One endpoint of the intended fold segment as a 2D coordinate.

required_fields.name: crease_line_edge2
required_fields.description: The other endpoint of the intended fold segment as a 2D coordinate.

required_fields.name: source_point
required_fields.description: A 2D coordinate on the moving side to be brought toward target_point.

required_fields.name: target_point
required_fields.description: The intended 2D position or x-y projection of source_point after the operation.

required_fields.name: source_layers
required_fields.description: Visible layers to move at source_point, e.g., ["all"], [1], or [1, 2].

required_fields.name: theta
required_fields.description: A non-negative fold angle in degrees.

additional_conventions: Commands are interpreted sequentially.
After each accepted command, subsequent commands refer to the updated observation.
Invalid parameter types, ranges, or values return executable: 0.
If executable is 0, the physical state is unchanged.
All points are interpreted in the current normalized view coordinate system.
The current x-y plane is the image plane of the current view; the z-axis points toward the viewer.
fold and make_crease take a non-empty list of fold_infos.
All fold_infos in one command are interpreted in the same pre-command observation.
Multiple fold_infos represent simultaneous folds and should be used only for folds intended to occur simultaneously.
A fold or make_crease command is atomic: if any fold_info is invalid or the fold_infos are physically incompatible, executable is 0 and the physical state is unchanged.
Each fold_info must specify a visible foldable region that lies on, or is sufficiently aligned with, the current x-y plane.
The alignment tolerance is defined by the simulator; rotate_3d must be used first if the intended fold region is not sufficiently aligned.
crease_line_edge1 and crease_line_edge2 must be distinct points on the intended visible fold segment.
A fold_info is invalid if crease_line_edge1 and crease_line_edge2 do not define a valid visible fold segment aligned with the current x-y plane.
Fold segments in the same fold_infos list may share endpoints, but their interiors must not cross or overlap.
source_point represents the point held or moved by the robot.
source_point selects the moving side relative to the supporting line through crease_line_edge1 and crease_line_edge2.
A fold_info is invalid if source_point lies on or too close to the supporting line of its fold segment.
A fold_info is invalid if the selected moving side or layer stack is not visible or not sufficiently aligned.
target_point specifies the intended result of moving source_point in the pre-command coordinate system.
For theta = 0, source_point is moved to target_point within the current x-y plane.
For theta = 0, crease_line_edge1 and crease_line_edge2 specify the crease or hinge segment associated with the in-plane movement.
For theta = 0, fold_type specifies the intended crease orientation if a crease is formed, but it does not define an out-of-plane rotation.
For theta = 0, a fold_info is invalid if source_point cannot be moved to target_point in the current x-y plane using the specified crease segment.
For theta > 0, target_point specifies the intended x-y projection of the folded source_point.
For 0 < theta < 180, the actual folded source_point lies on the vertical line through target_point; its z-position is determined by theta and fold_type.

Given crease_line_edge1, crease_line_edge2, source_point, target_point, theta, and fold_type, the simulator checks geometric consistency. If the resulting source_point position or projection is not sufficiently close to target_point, the fold_info is invalid.
Layer indices are local, viewpoint-dependent, 1-based indices counted from top to bottom in the visible stack.
source_layers are counted at source_point before the command is executed.
Invisible internal layer ordering does not need to be inferred.
For layer-specific selection, source_point should be placed on the moving side at or near a visible edge where the layer count can be visually determined.
source_layers: [1, 2] moves the first and second visible layers from the top; source_layers: ["all"] moves all visible layers in the selected stack.
Unless otherwise permitted, source_layers should be ["all"] or a contiguous prefix such as [1], [1, 2], or [1, 2, 3].
theta is a non-negative fold angle in degrees; theta = 0 represents an in-plane movement, and theta = 180 represents a full flat fold.
fold_type is either valley or mountain.
For theta > 0, fold_type is defined relative to the current top visible surface of the selected stack.
For theta > 0, a valley fold makes the current top visible surface become the inner side of the fold; a mountain fold rotates in the opposite direction.
fold executes all fold_infos as one simultaneous fold.
The resulting layer order after fold is determined by the simulator under physical consistency constraints.
make_crease temporarily folds or moves according to all fold_infos, returns the affected regions to the pre-operation configuration, and preserves the crease marks.
insert is a local layer-order operation, not a detailed physical insertion trajectory.
insert assumes that the flap has already been folded to the insertion location.
flap_point selects the visible flap, and pocket_point selects the visible insertion location or layer gap.
Insertion destination layer indices are counted at pocket_point in visible top-to-bottom order.
destination.mode must be either "under_layer" or "between_layers".
"under_layer" inserts the flap immediately under the specified visible layer.
"between_layers" inserts the flap between adjacent visible layers upper_layer and lower_layer, with upper_layer above lower_layer.
insert is executable only when the flap, insertion location, and destination layer relation are visible, local, and physically reachable.
insert returns executable: 0 if the destination is invalid, requires invisible internal layer reasoning, non-local rearrangement, or physically impossible layer crossing.
rotate_3d physically rotates the entire origami model around its current center of mass, not around the coordinate origin.
rotate_3d applies extrinsic rotations about the fixed axes of the current view before rotation, in x, y, z order.
rotate_3d uses the right-hand rule for positive rotation around each axis.
After rotate_3d, the current view coordinate system and visible layer order are reinterpreted from the updated observation.
finish terminates the simulation if executable is 1.

commands.command_description: Execute one or more fold_infos as a simultaneous fold.

required_parameters.name: fold_infos
required_parameters.description: A non-empty list of fold_info objects.

commands.command_description: Create crease marks by temporarily folding or moving according to one or more fold_infos and returning to the pre-operation configuration.

required_parameters.name: fold_infos
required_parameters.description: A non-empty list of fold_info objects.

commands.command_description: Locally insert a folded visible flap under or between visible layers by changing the local layer order.

required_parameters.name: flap_point
required_parameters.description: A 2D coordinate on the visible flap to be inserted.

required_parameters.name: pocket_point
required_parameters.description: A 2D coordinate near the visible insertion location or layer gap.

required_parameters.name: destination
required_parameters.description: The target layer relation. Use mode "under_layer" or "between_layers".

commands.command_description: Physically rotate the entire origami model around its center of mass.

required_parameters.name: theta_x
required_parameters.description: Rotation around the current x-axis in degrees, in [-180, 180].

required_parameters.name: theta_y
required_parameters.description: Rotation around the current y-axis in degrees, in [-180, 180].

required_parameters.name: theta_z
required_parameters.description: Rotation around the current z-axis in degrees, in [-180, 180].
